dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond for a breathing problem at a residence in [CITY].
Hello, I'm EMT Alex. Can you tell me what's happening with the patient?
She was having trouble breathing and then collapsed unconscious about 30 minutes ago.
Unresponsive, GCS score of 3-no eye opening, verbal, or motor response.
Airway preparation started-patient unable to maintain airway, planning to insert King LT/I-gel now.
Initial respiration rate is 12, not spontaneous at this time.
King LT/I-gel airway successfully placed and secured.
Pulse oximetry is reading 0% at this time.
No spontaneous breathing-BVM ventilation started with high-flow oxygen.
No pulse detected at arrival; patient pulseless.
Blood pressure is 0/0, consistent with a pulseless state.
Preparing to start intraosseous access now for medication delivery.
EKG shows asystole as of 06:09 AM initially.
Activating four-lead ECG monitoring as per protocol 2-7.
Epinephrine 1mg administered via intraosseous access as per protocol for cardiac arrest.
EKG still shows no organized activity-in asystole as of 06:10.
Glucose measurement could not be obtained due to patient access and condition constraints.
Autopulse CPR device is in use, normal saline running via the intraosseous site now.
Naloxone 2.0 mg given intraosseously per standard resuscitation protocols.
EKG now shows pulseless ventricular tachycardia, 210 BPM as of 06:34.
Delivering 200 joules defibrillation in manual mode as per protocol for conversion.
EKG briefly shows sinus rhythm at 06:35, followed by a return to asystole.
The patient has a history of stroke (CVA) due to infarction and is on regular renal dialysis.
She experienced respiratory distress in the sitting room, lost consciousness within minutes, then EMS was called at around 06:10 AM.
Protocol 2-7 initiated for cardiac arrest based on ECG readings and clinical indicators.
EKG now shows pulseless VT with 210 BPM, 8 respirations per minute, BP 0/0, and 0% saturation.
Performing second defibrillation at 150 joules-monitoring for rhythm conversion or spontaneous activity.
Rhythm now shows agonal/idioventricular activity with some periodic return to asystole.
Spontaneous respiration now observed at 21 per minute with increasing oxygen saturation into 90s range.
Rhythm shows 117 BPM, 142/86 BP, 94% saturation-temporary evidence of return of spontaneous circulation.
Administering another 1mg epinephrine push via IO at 06:50-repeat per protocol at every 3-5 rounds.
Patient was not experiencing pain before collapse, as reported by her family and confirmed by absence of pain response.
Capnography readings are being monitored alongside continuous airway management.
Destination is [COMPANY_NAME]-direct transport with full resuscitation ongoing per protocol.
Activating hot transport with lights and sirens-prioritizing time-sensitive care at the ED.
EKG currently mixed pulses, no sustained rhythm and no return of spontaneous circulation since 06:49.
Preparing next push of epinephrine at 07:01-respiratory support and airway remain functional.
Patient log-rolled onto the Reeves system and secured for transport-safe movement from scene is complete.
Pre-alert given to [COMPANY_NAME]-direct hot transfer ongoing with no further delays.
Epi 1mg again given at 07:01-continuing resuscitation per protocol.
Patient secured in supine position-transport remains unchanged.
Continuing transport with full resuscitation in progress-ED cares have been confirmed.
Patient has no reported or observed pain-documenting consistent with GCS 3.
Capnography remains in use to monitor airway and ventilation during transport.
Patient remains fully secured-ongoing resuscitation during transit to [COMPANY_NAME].
Proceeding with no stop at other facilities-direct transport to [COMPANY_NAME] for critical care.
Rhythm is again asystole with no signs of circulation-still no improvement observed.
Full cardiac arrest care continued using protocol 2-7 with no adjustment at present.
Continuous BVM with O2 and IO line functional-resuscitation ongoing during transport.
Patient condition remains critical-maintaining hot transport mode for rapid ED delivery.
